channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Millions across the US brace for plummeting temperatures and winter storms
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Residents across the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine are bracing for dangerously low temperatures as tens of millions of residents along the East Coast contend with a thick blanket of snow — and more snowfall in the forecast.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Poland wants the EU focused on security. Its border with Belarus highlights the challenges
item.description: POLOWCE, Poland (AP) — Poland's six-month presidency of the European Union is firmly focused on security. As Europe’s biggest land war in decades rages, fewer places highlight the challenges and contradictions of defending the bloc and its values more starkly than the border with Belarus.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: As ceasefire takes hold, Hamas returns 3 Israeli hostages and Israel frees 90 Palestinian prisoners
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — After 15 months of collective grief and anxiety, three Israeli hostages left Hamas captivity and returned to Israel, and dozens of Palestinian prisoners walked free from Israeli jail, leaving both Israelis and Palestinians torn between celebration and trepidation as the ce
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: From backing a ban to being hailed as a savior: Inside Trump's TikTok shift
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — During his first term as president, Donald Trump led the effort to ban TikTok, the hugely popular video-sharing site he said posed a threat to U.S. national security. But on the eve of his return to the White House, the president-elect is being hailed as the app's savior.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Couple with fake fire truck busted for impersonating firefighters near Palisades Fire in Los Angeles: sheriff
item.description: A couple from Oregon was busted for impersonating firefighters near the Palisades Fire in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The rise
item.description: The possibility of the U.S. outlawing TikTok kept influencers and users in anxious limbo during the four-plus years that lawmakers and judges debated the fate of the video-sharing platform. The moment its fans dreaded arrived late Saturday when the platform went dark in response to a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Heavy snow and frigid Arctic blast put 70 million across the US under winter storm warnings
item.description: Tens of millions of residents along the East Coast are bracing for several inches of snow Sunday followed by dangerously cold temperatures that will grip much of the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man admits to setting congressman's district office on fire over TikTok ban: police
item.description: Police said a Wisconsin man admitted to setting a congressman's district office on fire after the popular social media app TikTok went dark in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Over 100 out-of-state homeless in Hawaii sent back to mainland families
item.description: Hawaii lawmakers claim programs from other states send homeless to the Islands, especially in the winter.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Moore, KREX 5

item.title: Heavy snow and frigid Arctic blast put 70 million across the US under winter storm warnings
item.description: Tens of millions of residents along the East Coast are bracing for several inches of snow Sunday followed by dangerously cold temperatures that will take hold in much of the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Biden pardons the late Black nationalist Marcus Garvey as well as 4 others and commutes 2 sentences
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday posthumously pardoned Black nationalist Marcus Garvey, who influenced Malcolm X and other civil rights leaders and was convicted of mail fraud in the 1920s.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok says it's in the process of restoring service to US users
item.description: Millions of TikTok users in the United States were no longer able to watch or post videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular video-sharing app took effect Sunday amid questions over how long the blackout might last.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stephen Curry considered day to day after injuring left ankle in win over Wizards
item.description: Stephen Curry injured his left ankle in the final minutes of the Golden State Warriors victory over Washington on Saturday night and is considered day to day.
item.dc:creator: ERIC HE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texans' Kris Boyd flings helmet, gets penalized and then shoves his coach on first play vs. Chiefs
item.description: Texans cornerback Kris Boyd nearly shoved Houston special teams coach Frank Ross to the ground after the opening kickoff of their divisional playoff loss to the Kansas City Chiefs on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok restores service for US users based on Trump's promised executive order
item.description: TikTok says it’s restoring service to users in the United States just hours after the popular video-sharing platform went dark in response to a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Most voters in new survey seek toned-down Trump agenda
item.description: Over 60% of Americans polled disagree with Trump's idea to replace thousands of career civil-service workers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Extreme cold forecast for much of US in coming days
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — Another blast of winter weather is expected in parts of the U.S. in the coming days, including bone-chilling wind in the Northern Plains and unusual snow and ice in the Gulf Coast area. Cold weather forecasted for Monday for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago prepares for deportation arrests targeting hundreds next week after Trump takes office
item.description: Federal immigration officers will target more than 300 people with histories of egregious, violent crimes after President-elect Donald Trump takes office Monday, an official said, marking his administration's initial attempt toward fulfilling his promise of large-scale deportations.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC mayor defends private meeting with incoming President Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City's Democratic mayor, who faces federal bribery charges, is defending his private meeting with President-elect Donald Trump as the Republican prepares to retake the White House on Monday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he will ‘most likely’ give TikTok 90-day extension
item.description: (The Hill) — President-elect Trump told NBC News on Saturday that he is likely to give TikTok a 90-day extension while his incoming administration assesses the situation following the Supreme Court decision to uphold a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: One dead after early Saturday morning shooting in Ohio
item.description: A sergeant on the scene confirmed to NewsNation affiliate WDTN that the death is being investigated by the homicide unit.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, NewsNation

item.title: On LA fire lines, inmates shoulder heavy packs and tackle dangerous work for less than $30 a day
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Sal Almanza has worked 24-hour shifts in grueling terrain, cutting fire lines and hauling away brush trying to keep ahead of fires that have devastated several Los Angeles neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden got an Oval Office letter from Trump and may leave one in the desk himself. It’d be a first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ronald Reagan probably didn't realize he was starting a tradition when he wrote a note congratulating his successor and left it in the Oval Office desk drawer after two terms as president. He did that for George H.W. Bush, his successor and vice president of eight years.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vermont accused in lawsuit of tracking pregnant women considered unsuitable to be mothers
item.description: Vermont’s child welfare agency used baseless allegations to secretly investigate a pregnant woman and win custody of her daughter before the baby's birth, according to a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida hotel discriminated against Arab American group after Hamas' Oct. 7 attack against Israel, DOJ says
item.description: A Florida hotel owner reached an agreement with the DOJ, which accused the hotel of discrimination after it canceled a planned conference for an Arab American group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Homes were burning and roads already jammed when Pacific Palisades evacuation order came, AP finds
item.description: The first evacuation order covering neighborhoods closest to the start of the devastating Pacific Palisades wildfire didn’t come until about 40 minutes after some of those homes were already burning, according to an Associated Press analysis of emergency communications and interviews with survivors.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, GENE JOHNSON, CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, CLAUDIA LAUER and AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden admin drops its 'zero tolerance' policy targeting gun dealer licenses over paperwork errors
item.description: The Biden administration has reversed a policy revoking the federal firearms license of gun shops for simple paperwork errors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wisdom of a California grandmother, 90, goes viral amid Eaton Fire tragedy
item.description: A nearly 90-year-old California woman who lost her home to the Eaton Fire is going viral for pragmatism and positivity in the face of so much adversity.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Engineer Tedesco brothers use retrofitted RV to track UAPs
item.description: Two brothers have retrofitted an RV into a mobile lab to collect data about UAPs and other mysterious flying objects.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ingle, NewsNation

item.title: Biden got an Oval Office letter from Trump and may leave one in the desk himself
item.description: Ronald Reagan probably didn't realize he was starting a tradition when he wrote a note congratulating his successor and left it in the Oval Office desk drawer after two terms as president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bruce Willis shakes hands with first responders in rare public appearance
item.description: Amid the devastating wildfires in Southern California, Bruce Willis took time to thank first responders.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: PHOTOS: 'Home Alone' house in Illinois sells for $250K over asking price
item.description: A famous house featured in the iconic holiday film "Home Alone" sold this week for nearly $300,000 over its asking price.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, ABC 4

item.title: Top music acts announced for FireAid, a concert to support L.A. wildfire victims
item.description: The biggest names in pop, rock and country are coming together to raise funds for victims of the Palisades and Eaton wildfires and assist with the recovery.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — More than a week into what could be the costliest wildfires in U.S. history, weary and anxious Los Angeles-area residents were hoping for a favorable shift in the winds expected Thursday and an end to the constant fear that their lives might be further upended.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
